Hey, what's up everybody? I just wanted to walk you through the signup process. There's a lot of pieces to the signup process if you're doing it right. So don't get intimidated by it. It's just the name of the game if you want to really keep them, wow them, and retain them. Doing more things with the right intention will ultimately just make you more and improve their experience. A lot of this stuff can be automated though, and it looks like a lot of steps, but they're not hard to do. They've signed up for their front-end contract. Make sure you add them to the master roster, which is like this roster here. Now, after you've signed them up under that, under intro offer, put their name here, real simple, then you're going to add them to the clipboard system, which is really simple for intros, and that way you can see them all visually. After that, you're going to add them to skip you under a trial tag, so a trial group. That way you can mass text everyone so you don't have to like hand key everyone in. It'll make it a little bit easier. Now, if you want to stick with Vox you can, because we'll add them in the skibbio later, it's up to you. But I recently talked to a gym owner who had a lot of success using it this way, so I'm open to either one. If you want, you can skip this, but you're going to have to add them to skip you either way, so it might be easier. After that, now for whatever reason, they said we love you, and they want to sign up. So preferably do an e-contract. Waiver King is great for that if you want to use one. Otherwise, just have them sign up, paper, contract, and person. After they've signed their paper contract, have the WoW portal glued, have a tablet that's glued or chained down to your desk so that they immediately sign up on the WoW portal, which will then begin all of the automation for the shock and all. So all that stuff's automated. Then all you got to do is just go back to this continuity sheet, and you're going to move their name from here. Oops, there it is. Over to here. Real simple, right? And then at this point, you'll give them a team. So this might be like, easy, right? Okay, so you're going to add them to the team, and then you're going to write a handwritten welcome card to those people, and you'll put it in the mail. You can either use an app called Feld, which is really awesome. So what you do is you take a picture of them, and if you guys are giving thumbs up or getting excited, and then you can actually literally do it in two minutes, you can have them have a card sent to them, which is just awesome and exciting anyways. Or you can just do a handwritten card. The Fall card's really sweet because you don't have to go to the mail locks. It's like three bucks or 270 if you buy in bulk, which you might as well. But so it's not too bad. Anywho, after that, you're going to add them to the Skibbio group under Active EFTs. That way they can get the text updates and all of the promotions that you have for your Active Customer List. And then add them to your email list if they haven't already been added. Typically, if you have like an e-contract here, it'll automatically be synced. But if it hasn't, then make sure that they get it, the email list. And that pretty much finishes the signup process. This whole process right here is just the cancellation process. So it's real simple. And we have the whole breakdown for how to do that, and I'll do that in the next video.
